title: Spicy Juicy Thai Linguine Pasta
description: A crowdpleasing, lowsodium Thai-inspired linguine that bursts with spicy chili, fresh herbs, and a juicy coconutsoy sauce, perfect for a quick dinner.
instructions: Cook linguine in salted water according to package instructions until al dente. Drain, reserving 1/4 cup pasta water, and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sliced chili; sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook, turning occasionally, until pink and opaque, about 2–3 minutes (internal temperature 63 °C).
Pour in coconut milk and low‑sodium soy sauce; stir to combine.
Add brown sugar, basil, and cilantro; simmer for 3–4 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Return the cooked linguine to the skillet, tossing to coat with the sauce. If needed, add a splash of reserved pasta water to reach desired consistency.
Stir in lime juice and adjust seasoning with a pinch more soy sauce if necessary.
Divide the pasta among four plates, placing a lime wedge on each.
Serve immediately, encouraging guests to squeeze fresh lime for extra brightness.
Enjoy your spicy, juicy Thai linguine while it’s hot!
